methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Prusa Research, a bootstrapped manufacturer in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector, presents a limited peer-comparable valuation landscape. The broader sector comprises one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private peers including 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics. However, traditional peer-median triangulation is inapplicable here: of twelve same-sector companies identified, zero qualify as same-stage comparables, zero as adjacent-stage peers, and zero as similar-valuation-tier matches—falling short of the minimum three-datapoint threshold required for meaningful median analysis.

The absence of narrow-filter peer data reflects Prusa's distinctive position as a bootstrapped operator in a sector typically characterized by venture-backed entrants. Deploying all-sector median methodology would introduce material distortion, given the heterogeneity in capital structures, business models, and growth trajectories across the broader robotics universe. Public-market data from 277810.KQ offers limited directional insight without clear operational overlap.

Given these constraints, valuation of Prusa Research cannot be supported through standard peer triangulation methods. Alternative approaches—such as revenue multiple analysis if sufficient margin and growth data exist, or precedent-transaction comps within the 3D printing and manufacturing automation subsector—would be required to establish a defensible range. The key uncertainty remains the absence of appropriate bootstrapped-stage comparables in robotics manufacturing, precluding quantitative synthesis at this time.
